title: Astrocyte-Conditioned Media (ACM) Cultures
author: Elena Coccia
materials: Every 4 days, ACM will be harvested.

steps.title: Astrocytes will be cultured in Mature...
steps.instruction: Astrocytes will be cultured in Mature Astrocyte Media for ACM generation.

steps.title: Prepare 500 mL of Mature Astrocyte Media
steps.instruction: Prepare 500 mL of Mature Astrocyte Media.

steps.title: Seed astrocytes at a density of...
steps.instruction: Seed astrocytes at a density of 80,000 cells/mL in 100 mm plates.

steps.title: Remove the existing media from the flasks
steps.instruction: Remove the existing media from the flasks.

steps.title: Add fresh Mature Astrocyte Media to each flask
steps.instruction: Add fresh Mature Astrocyte Media to each flask.

steps.title: Incubate for an additional 4 days. - Duration
steps.instruction: Incubate for an additional 4 days. - Duration: 4 days - Notes: Ensure that the media is not disturbed during incubation.
1. After 4 days, collect the conditioned media from each flask into sterile 15 mL centrifuge tubes. 2. Centrifuge at 2000 x g for 5 minutes to remove cellular debris.
1. Transfer the supernatant to new sterile tubes and store at -80°C.

steps.title: ACM can be used to maintain...
steps.instruction: ACM can be used to maintain D25 midbrain organoids or 2D neuronal cultures.
1. Maintain organoids or neuronal cultures in ACM for 30 days. 2. Perform 50% media exchanges every 3 days. - Duration: 30 days

description: Incubate at 37°C with 5% CO2 until confluent. Notes: Monitor for confluence and replace media every 4 days.
